titleStmt.title: Letter from Hastings Dare Draper to Matthew Nathan, 29 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Hastings Dare Draper

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A Letter from solicitor Hastings Dare Draper (b. 1874) to Sir Matthew Nathan. In this letter Draper, on behalf of the Rathmines-based Rector Reverend Ernest H. Lewis-Crosby, is bringing to Nathan's attention a Dublin newspaper named 'Eye-Opener'. He complains about libelous comments against Lewis-Crosby and some of the members of his Rathmines Parish Church. Draper says the accusations are without merit. He calls for Nathan to act against the paper using the Defence of the Realm Act, and suppress its publication.Sir Matthew Nathan (1862 - 1939) was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.The letter refers to The Defence of the Realm Act (DORA). This act was passed in the United Kingdom on 8 August 1914. It gave the government wide-ranging powers during the war period, such as the power to requisition buildings or land needed for the war effort, or to make regulations creating criminal offences.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5561/4

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
5, St. Andrew Street Dublin 29th March 1916 Sir, The Revd. E. H. Lewis-Crosby of 'Hatherton', Mill-
town, Co. Dublin, Rector y of Holy Trinity Church, Rathmines, has
consulted me in reference to matter appearing in the current
number of a paper called 'The Eye Opener'. I enclose the publication in question and have
marked in blue pencil the matters complained of. I understand
that other matter of a somewhat similar nature has appeared in
the issue previous to the enclosed. It is common knowledge both in Rathmines Parish
and also in the City of Dublin that my client is the Clergyman
referred to and that the other peop ole referred to are members
of his Congregation. Mr Lewis-Crosby has pointed out to me that the
matter complained of, in addition to causing keen distress to the
members of the Congregation referred to, is, if permitted to con-
tinue, likely to bring his Church into disrepute. My client, apart from the attacks made upon him and
the members of his Congregation, feels that the whole tone of the
publication in question is of such a nature that grave injury may
be caused to the younger members of his Congregation into whose
hands it may chance to come, especially as he has been advised
that it is bought and sold openly not only in the Newsagents shops
but in the street of Dublin. I, therefore, respectfully ask you to bring this
letter and enclosure before the Government in the hope that,
taking all the circumstance into consideration, they may be
pleased to order the suppression of the paper or take such
steps as may prohibit in any future publication any similar
scandalous statements and infamous suggestions as those com-
plained of. I have the honour to be, Sir
Your obediant servant, Hastings D. Draper The Under-Secretary, Dublin Castle.
